keyPoints: Perfect information: All players have complete knowledge of the game state and previous moves.
Extensive games: The game tree is infinite, and all possible moves and outcomes are considered.
Minimax algorithm: A recursive algorithm used to find the optimal move by considering the maximum and minimum possible outcomes.
Abstraction: Simplifying the game tree by ignoring irrelevant moves and focusing on the most relevant ones.
Pruning: Removing branches of the game tree that will not affect the outcome.
Alpha-beta pruning: A variation of pruning that uses an upper and lower bound to optimize the search.
Nash equilibrium: A concept in game theory where no player can improve their payoff by unilaterally changing their strategy.
